transcript: Now let us test your legal knowledge. Again, we repeat that these are just examples for a basic understanding of what the law sets. For your individual case, please contact a legal clinic such as the South Asian Legal Clinic of Ontario at 416-487-6371 or the barbara schleifer commemorative clinic at 416-323-9149 question six after her husband sponsored her nina came from bangladesh to canada as a permanent resident pr her husband is a canadian citizen five months after she arrived nina's husband became physically and emotionally abusive nina wants to leave the marriage but her husband told her that if she does that she will lose her PR and be deported. Nina lose her PR in this case answer Nina will not lose her PR or get deported just because she leaves the marriage once a person is a PR they cannot lose their status for leaving a marriage or relationship however Nina's husband may take revenge by telling immigration authorities that she quote-unquote misrepresented or lied on her application and married him only to come to Canada. In this case, Nina's status can be at risk if IRCC investigates her. But she still has rights to defend herself and should speak to a lawyer. Question 7. Aisha got married to her husband in Pakistan. He is a permanent resident of Canada. After the wedding, her husband went back to Canada. A few months later, Aisha came to Canada on a visitor visa. After she arrived, her husband applied for her PR application. But while her application was processing, the couple's relationship deteriorated. Her husband constantly fought with her, neglected her, and had an affair. One day, Aisha decided to leave her husband after she left she found out that her husband withdrew her PR application what will happen to Aisha status in Canada answer if her visitor visa is still valid Aisha will still have temporary status in Canada, but she should renew it before it expires. Even if the visa expires, Aisha will have 90 days to restore the visa. If her visa has expired and the restoration period is gone, Aisha will not have any status, and she should speak to a lawyer. She may be able to apply for a humanitarian and compassionate application or a temporary resident permit. And finally, question eight. Mary's husband came from Sri Lanka on a two-year work permit. Mary came with her husband on a spousal work permit, which means her work permit is attached to her husband. A year after she arrived, Mary's husband told her he wanted a divorce. Will Mary lose her status in Canada? Answer. Mary will not lose her status because her marriage has ended. Her work permit will be valid until her husband's work permit is valid but if her passport expires before that her work permit will be valid until her passport is valid after her work permit expires mary cannot renew the work permit she has to consult a lawyer to speak about her options of staying in Canada. She may be able to apply for a humanitarian and compassionate application.
